caption: Figure 20.1 - Coding Agent Harness Loop. A model score or workflow result is produced by an issue, repository context, scaffold, tools, tests, retries, and review.
story_purpose: Shows why coding-agent performance is a system loop rather than a raw model number.
blocked_leap: Do not claim best coding model, productivity, or safety from a benchmark score unless scaffold and outcome evidence are named.

caption: Figure 20.2 - Coding Benchmark Caveat Matrix. SWE-bench, LiveCodeBench, terminal tasks, and product metrics each license different claims.
story_purpose: Lets the chapter use benchmark drama while preserving version, scaffold, date, and productivity blockers.
blocked_leap: Do not use benchmark rows as live rankings or enterprise-outcome evidence.

caption: Figure 20.3 - Tool-Call Lifecycle. A coding agent proposes an action, crosses a permission boundary, executes a tool, observes output, retries, and returns a diff for review.
story_purpose: Makes the action boundary visible and connects Claude Code security docs to the chapter's agency argument.
blocked_leap: Do not claim permission prompts prove universal safety.

caption: Figure 20.4 - Productivity Claim Blocker Map. Workflow claims are allowed now; productivity, adoption, revenue, replacement, and enterprise-outcome claims need stronger evidence.
story_purpose: Prevents the chapter from laundering benchmark or vendor-doc evidence into business-outcome prose.
blocked_leap: Do not write productivity or adoption claims without task/population/timeframe/customer-side evidence.
